description: Renders a piecewise-defined function on a 2D graph with alternating expression-condition pairs. Each pair defines one piece: the expression to plot and the domain condition under which it applies.
utility: Plot a piecewise-defined function on a 2D graph

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph container to plot on

arguments.name: expression1
arguments.description: First piece expression as a quoted ASCII math string - e.g., "-x"

arguments.name: condition1
arguments.description: Domain condition for the first piece - e.g., "x < 0"

arguments.name: expression2
arguments.description: Second piece expression as a quoted ASCII math string - e.g., "x^2"

arguments.name: condition2
arguments.description: Domain condition for the second piece - e.g., "x >= 0"

alternates.description: Plot a two-piece piecewise function with alternating expression-condition pairs
Plot a three-piece piecewise function with more pairs
Piecewise function with custom stroke color

examples.expression:
G4 = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-1, 10), type(plotting))
piecewiseplot(G4, "-x", "x < 0", "x^2", "x >= 0", c(purple))

G6 = g2d(at(4,16), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-3, 3), type(plotting))
piecewiseplot(G6, "-1", "x < 0", "1", "x >= 0", c(brown))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 10), type(plotting))
piecewiseplot(G, "x + 2", "x < -1", "1", "-1 <= x <= 1", "x^2", "x > 1", c(blue))
